executive_summary: Your supply chain faces dual pressures: a China-centric geopolitical cluster (US-Taiwan-export controls) affecting EMS, batteries, and nicotine suppliers, compounded by unexplained equity crashes in critical European and US component makers (Infineon down 6.8%, Stora Enso down 5.3%). The geopolitical risks are material but somewhat known; the stock crashes are the real wildcard—they suggest either sector-wide margin compression, demand signals, or hidden operational issues that haven't yet hit headlines. Immediate actions: escalate with Infineon and Smoore (both critical exposure + sharp moves) to understand whether this is cyclical or structural, and stress-test your China EMS footprint (Huizhou BYD, Smoore) for Taiwan-linked supply gaps. South Africa and South Korea geopolitical exposure is real but slower-moving; use the next 4–6 weeks to map alternative substrates and filters before those regimes deteriorate further.
